notes: addmm fused assign + no-shift fast path, mx.compile, f16 assign
argsort+sorted scatter_add: 11% slower, argsort overhead dominates
eval every 10 iters instead of every iter: 1.9% faster
B=1 skip loop/stack in centroid update: no gain, compiled handles it
eval_every=25/50/100 all ~1.284s, no gain beyond eval_every=10
f32 addmm instead of f16: 5% slower, f16 throughput advantage is real
K-chunked assign K=250: 17% slower, loop+ops overhead > cache benefit
K-chunked assign K=500: 14% slower, same issue
custom Metal kernel argmax: 31% faster, mx.argmax was 5.8ms vs 2.2ms
vec4 loads + threadgroup 1024: marginal, commit for cleaner code
Metal atomic centroid update: 2.6x slower than scatter_add (contention)
2D matmul (squeeze batch): no gain in compiled pipeline
f32 scatter_add source (skip f16->f32 conversion): 2% faster
all iters inside one mx.compile call: 9% faster, cross-iter optimization
N-chunked 50K/100K/250K: all slower, matmul most efficient at full N
quantize+q8_matmul: quantization overhead (0.6ms) > matmul savings (0.5ms)
random projection D=128->64: 93% assignment mismatch, completely unusable despite 2x matmul speedup
simdgroup_matrix fused matmul+argmax: 2x slower than addmm+argmax (simd units << AMX hardware matmul)
mx.contiguous on transposed centroids: 2% faster addmm with contiguous memory layout
matmul + fused argmax+bias vs addmm + argmax: <0.5% diff, not worth the complexity
flat scatter_add (no per-batch loop): 45% faster standalone, same compiled; cleaner code
threadgroup 32 for Metal argmax: 5% faster argmax (1.67ms vs 1.75ms), 0.7% faster overall
cooperative (2/4/8 threads/row), multi-row, loop unroll x2/x4: all ~1.7ms, no gain, bandwidth-limited
quantized_matmul 4/8-bit: 7.3ms vs 6.2ms f16, slower due to dequant overhead; 4-bit only 83% match
K-tiled (125/250/500) matmul + running argmax: 13-15ms, 2x slower from multiple small matmuls
block_masked_mm top-2/4/6: 7.7-8.5ms, 13-52% match, block structure incompatible with NN search
f16 centroids (skip f32 cast): no gain, compiler overlaps cast with previous ops
(K,D)@(D,N) vs (N,D)@(D,K): 6.5ms vs 5.9ms, standard layout is optimal
2/4 N-splits to overlap addmm/argmax: 7.43ms vs 7.47ms, marginal from half-size overlap
custom Metal tiled matmul+argmax: 46ms vs 7.6ms, compute shaders 6x slower than AMX hardware matmul
bf16 addmm in compiled pipeline: 10.4ms/iter vs 7.65ms, bf16->f16 cast for argmax adds overhead
mx.qqmm mxfp8/nvfp4: NYI on M3 architecture, requires newer hardware
separate GPU streams for assign/update: 8.2ms vs 7.7ms uncompiled, stream overhead dominates
vec8 (1.74ms) and vec16 (1.73ms) loads vs vec4 (1.69ms): wider loads no gain, bandwidth-limited
K=64 padded to 128: 1.64ms vs 1.17ms/iter, padding overhead > matmul efficiency gain
3x scatter_add centroid update: same time as 1x, centroid update 100% overlapped with addmm
ensure_row_contiguous=False on Metal argmax: no gain in compiled pipeline, compiler knows addmm output is contiguous
